mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales raises expected near-term corporate revenue growth, causing higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the latest RSAFS growth rate is above its prior 3-month rate.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:latest RSAFS value is 366404.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of unemployment above 9% signals severe labor-market slack and imminent earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days after the threshold is breached.
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:latest UNRATE value is 9.9 as of t-1mo
regime_hint: recession or early post-shock labor-market stress
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations after UNRATE crosses above 9.0% produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or average triggered returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5% identifies a zero-lower-bound stress regime in which policy easing no longer offsets growth fears, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:latest FEDFUNDS value is 0.2 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day S&P 500 returns after FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.5% are positive in more than 60% of triggered cases or exceed unconditional 60-day returns by more than 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production over the prior month signals improving operating leverage and inventory demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclical earnings expectations adjust.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:latest INDPRO value is 102.2666 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly INDPRO changes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward cases or if the mean return difference versus negative INDPRO changes is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with falling unemployment confirms broad labor-income improvement, increasing consumption and reducing default risk, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is negative
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS
series:UNRATE
fact:latest PAYEMS value is 130161.0 as of t-1mo
fact:latest UNRATE value is 9.9 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-falling signal does not outperform either single-series signal over 20 trading days or if triggered returns are positive in less than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts rebound above 600 thousand units signals improving credit availability and construction demand, lifting cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:latest HOUST value is 626.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day S&P 500 returns following HOUST crossings above 600 are not higher than unconditional 60-day returns or if the triggered win rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI rises while the federal funds rate remains below 1%, real short rates fall, supporting equity valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change is positive and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:latest CPIAUCSL value is 217.729 as of t-2mo
fact:latest FEDFUNDS value is 0.2 as of t-1mo
regime_hint: financial repression with negative real-rate pressure
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI-rising and FEDFUNDS-below-1% interaction has a lower 20-day average S&P 500 return than periods with FEDFUNDS above 1% or if Rank IC is negative over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP over the latest reported quarter signals broad demand destruction that lowers expected aggregate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
fact:latest GDPC1 value is 13254.7 as of t-4mo
failure_condition: Reject if negative quarterly GDPC1 changes are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or if triggered returns are not below unconditional returns.
